description: Five explanation layers for adaptive learning. Each lesson can be rendered at multiple depths. Not separate lessons — layers within one lesson.

simple.label: Simple
simple.description: Explained like you're new to tech. Uses everyday analogies. Assumes zero cloud knowledge.

characteristics.detail: Breadth over depth — what and why, not how

core.label: Core
core.description: Standard cloud engineering explanation. Technical but accessible. The default level most learners use.

characteristics.detail: Balanced breadth and depth — how things work

professional.label: Professional
professional.description: Practical, production-oriented. Deep dives into configuration, trade-offs, and real-world patterns.

characteristics.detail: Deep configuration, CLI examples, API references

production.label: Production
production.description: War stories and battle-tested patterns. Incident reports, debugging narratives, monitoring setups, cost optimization.

characteristics.vocabulary: Full technical depth, assumes operational experience
characteristics.detail: Incident timelines, debugging traces, performance data

architect.label: Architect
architect.description: System-level thinking. Multi-cloud, organizational patterns, build-vs-buy decisions, 5-year horizon planning.

characteristics.detail: Architecture decision records, system trade-offs, organizational impact

frontmatter_declaration.description: Each lesson declares which levels are available in frontmatter

mdx_syntax.description: Content blocks are wrapped in conditional level tags
mdx_syntax.example:
:::level simple
Explain this like I'm five...
:::

:::level core
The standard explanation...
:::

:::level professional
Advanced configuration details...
:::

future_ui.description: Learners can toggle between levels with a dropdown/tabs. AI can auto-select the appropriate level based on learner profile.
features: Level selector dropdown in lesson header
Remember last chosen level in localStorage
AI auto-detection based on skill assessment
